review_text: We had a 2 b/r dog friendly cabin in early May and loved it, the park was quiet and staff very helpful. The weather was fine and sunny so made the most of the front deck which had some water views. The pool area also looked great. Shops are close by for all needs. Would definitely recommend staying here.

We stayed in a dog friendly 2 bed cabin for 3 nights. It was in a great spot with some views of the water. We had a great time exploring the surrounding area with Ulladulla shops close by for everything we needed. We were spoilt with warm sunny days, so the large deck was well used. The pool area looked lovely. Would definitely recommend this park.

Great staff.
Excellent location near town.

We always love coming to this park. The location is stunning, the facilities are fantastic, and the grounds are exceptionally well maintained.
We absolutely loved our most recent stay. Catching up with our extended family made it even more special, and we all had a wonderful time. The park managers were also outstanding as always! Thank you

Beautiful and peaceful place to stay. My family an I enjoy staying here I just wish the kids park was updated to something more modern (it’s something from when I was a kids) and that the jumping pillow had a shade cloth over it as my child burnt bottom of her foot one hot day when attempting to play. Also the put put which is very popular needs some tlc as the green is ripping at nearly every hole. I am a little surprised as I have stayed in both the Lake Conjola and Burrill Lake Holiday Havens and there kids areas by far out shine this one. With how many options there are out there these days keeping with these things are very important especially for families.

The park is lovely with spectacular views and very peaceful. However I found the play areas for the children to be outdated especially in comparison to the other Holiday Havens in the area. The jumping pillow really needs a cover as during sunny days my kids feet were getting burnt or blistered from how hot the pillow get so they couldn’t go in it. A the park is past its prime a more modern one would be so much nicer. Also the mini golf (which is very poplar) needs some tlc. Park has potential to be a 5 star amazing family friendly park just needs some tlc, which is surprising when you compare this parks equipment to Lake Conjola or Burrell Lake Holiday Haven.
The caravan site we got for Easter was amazing.  Their communication , location and cleanliness of the park was great.
We love camping here. Great location close to town. Good facilities especially the pool.
Loved this caravan park. Perched on the headland amongst the trees was really special.
Absolutely amazing holiday location, next to the water, amazing service and amenities. A perfect holiday destination.
Beautiful park. No crowds. Right on the water. Park managers are lovely. Safe for kids to do their own thing
Schöner Wohnmobilstellplatz in toller Lage mit Zugang zum Meer über eine Treppe. Die Sanitäranlagen sind leider in die Jahre gekommen und nicht besonders sauber bei unserem Besuch.

The toilets, showers, and campground kitchen need to be renovated. Unfortunately, they are a bit outdated.
The Wi-Fi coverage at the campground was very poor.
Good fishing

We could of overlooked the filthy front door and the rat faeces on the outdoor kitchen of our cabin as the pool, splash pad, jumping pillow and location of the park was good. Unfortunately we were charged for damage not caused by our dog. We didn't notice a mattress damaged by previous clients and have now been stung for the cost of someone else's carelessness. It was on the top bunk, somewhere our 14yr old arthritic dog could not reach. Park owner wouldn't listen to reason, a disappointing ending to a family weekend away. Check both sides of your mattress on check in.

Stayed 2 nights mid-January on a powered site. Site was large and amenities were clean.||Nice stroll down the stairs to the sea pool and was great to take the kids for a walk around the look out track.||Our area was sheltered from the wind which was also nice!||Didn't get to try the pool but the splash park was more than enough for the kids.||Staff were very pleasant to deal with.

We had to leave Holiday Haven Ulladulla early due to a medical emergency. My wife slipped on rocks nearby and broke her wrist, requiring urgent hospital treatment and a return to Sydney.

The park itself was acceptable and the facilities met basic needs. However, we were disappointed that no partial refund or credit was offered for the unused night, and by the lack of empathy shown despite multiple requests to the park management team.

Future guests may wish to consider travel insurance for unexpected events or consider other nearby holiday parks.

Frank X

Absolutely love,love Holiday Haven Ulladulla.
Chris & Rey are Absolutely fantastic.  They are so laid back & very helpful.
Great park from scenic sites,  fantastic pool, very clean facilities.
Loving our cliff top ocean views & waking to sounds of ocean & kookaburras
Catherine Mackenzie
Great caravan park to stay in

Yet another great holiday at Ulladulla Headland Caravan Park. Special thanks to Leanne. Always helpful and pleasant in assisting us. Thanks also to Chris who takes the time to make us feel welcome.|Looking forward to our next visit and have no reservation in recommending this park to other pet owners.

THIS IS AN EXTREMELY DANGEROUS PLACE! A combination of a high kerb and loose site matting caused the campervan to wheelspin. This whipped the matting from under the feet of my partner causing a fractured rib, fractured thumb and multiple pain and bruising. She could have fallen under the wheels of the van with even worse results! We spent an entire day at the nearby hospital and will also be inhibited for the next 6 weeks at least. And to add insult to injury, the Park refused to reimburse our out of pocket medical expenses, insinuating that it was our own fault!
THERE ARE PLENTY OF SAFER ALTERNATIVES NEARBY!
UPDATE 2 NOVEMBER:
The response from the Park suggesting we contact them  came a week AFTER we had written to them with a full description of the incident. Moreover, they have always had our telephone number! After the response, on 26 October we wrote again. We have confirmation that both mails have been received. But we have not received a response, not even an acknowledgement of receipt. This has all the hallmarks of a Park that wants to present an image to you, the reader, without backing anything up.
Really lovely spot and super lovely receptionist :)

Really good caravan park. We stayed in a caravan so cannot comment on the cabins but looked nice from the outside. Facilities are great, with a lovely pool and splash park, tennis court, jumping pillow and even lawn bowls! Great little private beach down some adventurous stairs, and a very short distance to Ulladulla shops for any supplies. Lovely and helpful staff. Bathroom amenities quite clean.
We have visited here several times and have always found everything to be done well. Today when we had to change booking Nicole was very understanding and helpful

Great little getaway overlooking the ocean. Close to shops
Lovely staff

This is an exceptional holiday park, very well located just a few minutes walk from the shopping strip, the beaches and the harbour at Ulladulla. It's clean and spacious with easy access caravan sites, excellent amenities and about the most  friendly and helpful staff I have encountered.  To top it off, the sites are pet friendly.  An excellent experience and not to be missed

The grounds and facilities were wonderful, the cabin was warm and cosy, we felt secure and our dogs had amazing amenities. Friendly staff, very helpful and the booking process was seamless. We'll be back!
One of the best holiday haven parks. Definitely a place to take the kids or grandchildren we will be back again
Amazing stay! Best holiday destination in Ulladulla. Leanne at reception was friendly and helpful!

Do yourself a favour and book this beautiful park. We stayed on powered site 120 which has uninterrupted ocean views. Lovely grassy site, very well maintained. Grounds boys and office staff went over and above. The ammenities are very clean also. Washing machines credit card operated, so convenient when you have run out of coins. There is a pool, playground, mini golf, and jumping pillow, great for kids. There is also a headland walk with stunning view’s within the property. I would give this park 10 stars if I could.

Lovely lady at check in
Nice park

What a Great location, the park layout was great for our stay in our family van over the Easter school holidays 2025 . The friendly staff where always happy to help, we had an en-suite site for the girls, I used the amenities, which where always very clean, a short walk for a swim was like we had our own private beach, the water was crystal clear! I would recommend staying here it was a very relaxing south coast stay
Had a wonderful stay at Holiday Haven Ulladulla. The location, staff and site were all 5 star. Will be going back!!
Beautiful location and amazing staff. Thanks for having us Chris and Rey!

Just spent Easter in the beautiful cabin number eight. Has great views good facilities and extremely clean. Thank you to Chris and the reception team who are always really helpful. We also enjoyed playing table tennis minigolf and bowls and really loved how sparkling and clean the big pool is. A great way to spend Easter.

We felt very fortunate that we were able to find a caravan spot over Easter at this Caravan park. Our spot 32A was perfect for us, it felt like a private retreat to us. Everywhere birds in trees and bushes, it was pleasant.
The park is well set up for families with kids, so many play areas and you offered special events over Easter. The bathrooms at the pool area are lovely set up, I just have one suggestion maybe a floor squeegee so that customers can clear the water when using the bathroom. Otherwise we found everything clean and well looked after. Friendly staff and lovely reception when we arrived.
They keep the rubbish bins in one place to easy for them, you have to walk long to reach them.

I’ve thought long and hard about this review, and concluded I must share the story.

We stayed 14-15 March 2025. 2 adults and 1 much younger single.

Great location and cabin was nice and clean.

BUT the cabin was an oven and the aircon was on the fritz.  The Manager came and ‘reset’ the unit via the fuse box - but it was basically only operating as a fan. Hot & unpleasant.  Next day a technician came but to no avail, and a portable unit showed up. It provided some relief but the NOISE was deafening.  There were no other 2 bedroom cabins available for us to move into. So we had 2 miserable nights. At $190/night for the pleasure.

And NO discount was offered or given by the manager.

Pretty shabby in my opinion.

We were part of a larger group and although we plan to return to Ulladulla, we will NOT be staying here again.

Additional notes to a lame response -

And I DID ASK FOR A DISCOUNT both at the time and upon checkout. Each time declined.

The substitute cabin offered was not practical or appropriate for a middle-aged couple and an 18 year old Japanese girl.
Excellent park. Very spacious.
Still friendly nice staff. Good location. Clean cabin.
Hidden gem right near town
We had a wonderful holiday at Holiday Haven Ulladulla.  The grounds and facilities were great and are kept immaculately.  The staff were friendly and helpful.

This Holiday park is very spacious with lots of cabins and caravan sites available.  Our cabin was nice and had everything we needed.  The women on the phone who booked us said we had booked The Cheapskate cabin which I dont understand.  She made feel cheap cause we booked this.|The  only downside was the lady on reception who everytime I called on the phone she was so very rude.|Would recommend for a weekend in either a cabin or caravan sites

Beautiful location! Short (but steep) walk to town.
Absolutely awesome pool. Such a good depth and a large wading area for toddlers.
Great parks and tidy, clean (but old) amenities. The sites are large. The lady at the front counter was efficient and polite.
No onsite coffee but that didn’t worry us. A great dog friendly park. Even a fenced off dog yard for toilet time (for the dogs). Putt putt, a bowling green, splash park. This park nearly had it all! Excellent stay. But only 4 stars due to steep location and we do like having an onsite cafe or coffee cart and kids club. But that would not put us off coming back. We loved our stay.

Very Poor Experience.

1. BBQs in the park didn’t work.
2. Rooms very small for Families
3. No area for groups to have a meal or cook together.
4. Staff member without ID or Uniform approaching and following Children.
5. Manager seems to think he is an agent for NSW Police.
6. Different park rules depending on who you ask.

Overall very poor experience will not return mabye this is why the park was half empty over a January weekend. Owners really need to review the type of people they hire to Manager their facilities.

The General town is lovely but find somewhere else to stay.

Thanks for reply to my review clearly we have a very different version of events. I can assure you I have never stayed at the park before, the BBQs were not working when I visited and I didn’t abuse anybody. Staff weren’t carrying ID or in uniform. I respect park rules and ensure compliance. Kids scoooter were in pool area next to me while I was supervising my children in the pool. Kids were not riding in the pool area.

Your camp kitchen BBQ Facilities weren’t upto scratch during my visit.

Clearly by your response Management don’t take feedback very well and would rather try to justify actions rather than than take ownership.

We just returned home from a 7 night stay (Xmas holidays)
The park and cabin was clean and tidy, staff were very friendly and go out of their way to help with whatever they can.
No nonsense or noise during the night which was a plus for us as we have young children.
The park is walking distance to beach and Main Street.
Highly recommend.
4 weeks til we arrive! It will be our 4th year at the park and can't wait! Cabins are great and staff are always so kind and helpful.
Clean cabins locally situated. Friendly staff

What a little gem !! Just had the pleasure of a few days here.  Astro turf site, spotlessly clean amenities  beautiful pool area. Played a couple of putt putt rounds (most holes on the slant, but fun anyway).  Walking distance to town,  sea pool etc. Wouldn't hesitate to recommend

Very convenient location--close to the shops, close to the major road, but nice and quiet. Friendly staff. The cabin was perfect for one person, or a couple with up to three children.  Management responded very quickly to  my report of a faulty toilet cistern (it was replaced with a new one within 24 hours and I was kept fully informed of progress).

This is my go holiday park in Ulladulla, I've stayed here several times in the cabins,  It's a beautiful park with it's own access to a beach and the customer service is a 11/10 the staff go over and beyond to make your stay perfect.

Good park. Location is close to town if you want to walk and about 1km to the Lighthouse lookout. (great morning we had watching whales). Price is $60pn. Our site was not flat but was the only one available not under a tree. Amenities or old but are clean and work fine. Has dump point and is pet friendly. Would consider staying again
Nice caravan park, location   very easy to find and easy access to shops and Ulladulla harbour.

We had a lovely stay at the Harbour View cabin. It was spacious, kids loved the spa bath and having the bbq on the verandah was very convenient. Great location, loved all the child-friendly facilities. We will definitely come back in the future and recommend to family & friends 😊
Great location and great van sites.

We've been coming here for years. It's always neat and tidy, although the bathrooms are only cleaned once a day on a long weekend, so it's not fantastic. They've done a lot of work on the van sites. Cabins are old but in good condition and quite roomy.

Love it here. Great location, excellent amenities and every year we return it’s a consistent service that doesn’t let down.
Thank you Chris for a holiday experience we rely on for the young kids.
Coming back next year and the one after!

Best of all the Holiday Haven Parks! Management team are super friendly and very helpful. Always checking in with guests around the park and assisting with anything you need.
Extremely clean amenities and great activities for the kids. Great location close to Ulladulla shops. Will be back every year!

This is a great campsite.  Staff are very helpful and friendly.  The view from our cabin is excellent. Can't wait to come back. Close to main drag and everything is pretty much in Walking distance. Back again, actually its our 4th since 1st review. We are in Cabin 9 and boy is it great. The Shower is the GOAT. Used the bbq on our verandah yesterday and its a beauty. Its a Fantastic Place and The staff from reservation to check-in are Really Good. Nothing a problem.
Loved the park and the cabins we had a large group and were all made to feel welcome including the dogs

Lady in the office is extremely rude, try’s to talk down on campers as if she is better then her guests who are paying her wage. i will never stay here again as long as she is working here. Also facilities were absolutely disgusting and poorly maintained

We stayed in a caravan and a tent on a powered ensuite site with our dog.  The amenities were great with the pool,park and beach a short walk away.. What was really stupid is that we came down in 2 cars and had quite alot of gear as we were staying 3 nights and had a tent for myself and disabled son. There is a 1 car policy and the owners don't budge on that at all. So U can't even drive in just to drop off the camping gear and bags etc.. I didn't mind not being able to park there but to not even allow people to drop off there gear is a bit ridiculous especially when our site was the opposite end of the very large caravan park.. we had to unload the contents of my car into the caravan which meant unloading the stuff in there car into the caravan aswell just so we could get a ride to the site and then unpack again.. then we had to do it all again 4 days later when we packed up. It's a Really silly policy and like I said I would have happily drove back out and parked on the street after unloading the car but was not allowed to..looking at other reviews this seems to be a common complaint..

Lovely caravan park, located in Ulladulla. Older type with mature trees. Great venue for bowls, tennis,  table tennis, swimming in pools and even has a kiddies splash park. Plus bouncing cushion,  and climbing structure. Close to all town amenities.
Great place to stay and spend time with our furry friends
Well what can I say 😂

Lovely big site with synthetic turf,friendly staff.
No complaints here,nice and peaceful, were self contained so didn't use amenities.
There's plenty of play areas for kids also.
We would definitely visit again. Thanks holiday haven.

Wonderful park close to all facilities in Ulladulla. The grounds are spotless. Views of the beautiful bay between the trees and able to walk everywhere. Could hear the surf of a night. Clean facilities. Helpful staff. What's not to like?
Thoroughly enjoyed our stay and highly recommended.
Great c/van park family friendly staff friendly and helpful ,sites big enough for your van and car ,well maintained park plenty to see in the area

We have thoroughly enjoyed a lengthy stay at Holiday Haven in Ulladulla. There is an abundance of onsite activities for the kids, and it is within a convenient walking distance from town and the beach. The staff are exceptional with their genuine interest in making your stay enjoyable and comfortable. The kids and I always enjoyed our chats with Leanne and Di in the office! The amenities are spotless, the facilities clean and well maintained, and gardens beautiful. This park rates as one of the top parks we have stayed at, whilst on our trip of Oz. Well done to Chris and the team for such a well run, family friendly holiday park.

relpay to comment:
We came as a big family group looking for an enjoyable CAMPING experience, staying late night, camp fire, and movie nigh, and every other place my family and I have visited have given that experience and NO ONE "complained" except your camp site, especially when it came to complaining  about our RACE and how being ARAB was the reason for our behaviour, even when we were being loud we stoped the first time we got a complaint but you keep on bringing it up as the only excuse for our ‘behaviour’ when your establishment treated us with obviously biased treatment, singling us out from the rest of the gusts when there were others being worst then us
I don’t appreciate your mistreatment and rude behaviour towards us and you even went as far as threatening us with police reinforcements for being ‘too loud’, that in itself speaks lengths for you views of our ‘loud Arab group’ as you said yourself.
I’m mostly disappointed about the chaise down we felt throughout the entire trip and we weren’t able to enjoy it at all. I truly don’t recommend this place for large families group with kids.
+ if we were even ‘smoking’ within the cabins how would you have known, did you have cameras installed within the cabins, that’s a HUGE invasion of privacy and might need police interference.

We’ve just returned from a 3 night trip. Stayed in cabin 26 which was really spacious, nice deck area, comfortable beds- the triple bunk fit my 3 teenagers who are over 6ft tall. At 600$ for three nights at the beginning of school holidays for a family of 5, it’s great value.

The cabin was clean, nice crisp sheets, plenty of pillows and blankets and huge bath towels. Also provided plenty of plates and cutlery so we weren’t washing up after every meal. The park is great, only a short walk into town, the ocean pool and Warden Head bushwalks and beach. I imagine it would be even better for a family with young kids with the pool, waterpark, jumping pillow and playgrounds. We loved the proximity to several golf clubs, especially the 9 hole par three at Molloymook which is perfect if you don’t want to spend all day on the course.

I asked for a late check out which was approved free of charge as there was no one booked in the night we left.

My only quip is that the steps down to Lobster Jacks beach are closed. Obviously the park can’t do anything about that, but it shouldn’t be advertised as a feature of the park if it’s not available. We had hoped to go snorkelling there and it was part of the reason we picked Holiday Haven Ulladulla. I was told by another traveller that it’s been closed for years. Maybe put a note on the website to let people know.

I would definitely recommend this park and we’re looking forward to coming back.

Stayed a few nights at a cabin here, all in all great value for the price and would stay again.

Pros
- Excellent location, by the water and a stone's throw away from Ulladulla town center with restaurants, supermarkets, cafes.
- Cabins were very clean and well stocked with essentials. Showers were hot and everything worked as it should,  easily higher than country town motel standard.
- Cabins and campgrounds were well spaced out, had no issues with noisy neighbours at all.
- Great public facilities, pool was pristine, and the public kitchen and dining areas also well equipped.

Cons
- The receptionist I dealt with won't be winning any miss congeniality awards 2024, I've seen better service attitudes at the RMS. However I'd be mad to expect 5 star service at this price so she was fine.

Wanted to write a review for Holiday Haven Ulladulla as the review posted in March 2024 almost put me off booking. Couldn’t have had a more different experience. ||We’ve just returned from a 3 night trip. Stayed in cabin 26 which was really spacious, nice deck area, comfortable beds- the triple bunk fit my 3 teenagers who are over 6ft tall. At 600$ for three nights at the beginning of school holidays for a family of 5, it’s great value. ||The cabin was clean, nice crisp sheets, plenty of pillows and blankets and huge bath towels. Also provided plenty of plates and cutlery so we weren’t washing up after every meal. The park is great, only a short walk into town, the ocean pool and Warden Head bushwalks and beach. I imagine it would be even better for a family with young kids with the pool, waterpark, jumping pillow and playgrounds. We loved the proximity to several golf clubs, especially the 9 hole par three at Molloymook which is perfect if you don’t want to spend all day on the course. ||I asked for a late check out this morning which was approved free of charge as there was no one booked in tonight. ||My only quip is that the steps down to Lobster Jacks beach are closed. Obviously the park can’t do anything about that, but it shouldn’t be advertised as a feature of the park if it’s not available. We had hoped to go snorkelling there and it was part of the reason we picked Holiday Haven Ulladulla. I was told by another traveller that it’s been closed for years. Maybe put a note on the website to let people know. ||I would definitely recommend this park and we’re looking forward to coming back.
Lovely well set up park, central to shops and cafes
Only stayed a night on a powered site. Very windy and wet so didn't get to enjoy what looked like a wonderful place to spend some time.

This is a fabulous park. We stayed on an ensuite site , clean with a great shower. This park has everything, family friendly with every activity you can think of and dog friendly with some lovely walks. Trees and birds everywhere . We would definitely come again.

I just spent close to $600 for 2 cabins for 2 nights.|We are regular cabin users at various caravan parks and I would have to say this is the worse we have ever stayed in as far as broken items goes. ||Upon arrival I opened all windows as it was a hot day - the main bedroom window had a broken lock clip so I was unable to open it. Bathroom had broken tiles on the floor and in the shower. Cooktop was covered in painted over rust that the rust was showing through. Sliding screen door was absolutely filthy and very rattly and didn't want to close properly. Broken coat hanger sitting in the base of the wardrobe in bedroom 2. A dead rat on the road out the front of our cabin.|Also my daughter in law found several dead rats around her cabin.|Bird poo on all of the outdoor chairs. (which is just nature at its best but it should be cleaned in between guests)  And the most dangerous of all was broken switches on the power points in the kitchen. ||I spoke to reception (prior to knowing about the broken power switches) |they got maintenance to remove the dead rat and were not interested in the broken tiles as she said they already know about that. And she did say she would send someone to attend to the window lock however during our stay no one showed up to fix it. Mattresses are very old and lumpy. |We paid for a larger 2 bedroom cabin and the main bedroom was so small that we could hardly even walk up the sides of the bed and our bags had to stay on the lounge. (the bathroom was bigger than our bedroom) ||Parking for a second car is disgusting - they don't even have a visitors parking so my adult son had to leave his car out on the street (that is not good for all the local houses nor is it secure parking for the guests) ||Then on departure our boom gate code wouldn't work so I had to wait at the office while that got sorted.||Contents in the cabin were clean however maintenance leaves a lot to be desired. ||We had a family gathering and had booked 6 cabins in total and everyone had issues with their cabin. My brother in law booked the most expensive cabin in the park and his BBQ didn't work and they couldn't even fix it. ||Very disgusted with the level of maintenance and service.|Beach access staircase was shut - not sure how long that has been out of action for but there is a very pretty little beach down there that no one can get to.||My son’s cabin was directly opposite the bins so their view was “rubbish bins” that didn’t even have any type of screen around them and cars stopping constantly to dump their rubbish. Also on the deck of my sons cabin there was nothing under the deck railing to stop my 3 year old grandson from falling off the deck (no posts or wire cable)||Leanne was quite rude in reception and had very little interest in my issues.||It is a nice park and lots of activities for young and old - however the staff need to have more empathy especially when we have paid top dollar for cabins and maintenance really needs to be done on all cabins.

Stayed in a spa cabin for 2 nights, it had everything you could need in it and extremely clean. Beds were a little hard but ok.
The park had alot to offer for all ages, pool, splash zone, mini golf, lawn bowls, table tennis just to name a few and at not extra cost. Easy wall to the shops and beach from the well maintained park. All the staff were wonderful and very welcoming.
We will definitely stay again but next time we will make it alot longer!

Just spent a few days over the Australia day weekend, I cant fault the park , the service was delightful , the facilties were clean the kids  loved the splash park and the pool ... Will definatley do this again .

Love this park.
3yrs in a row now.
Cant fault it.
Would love to get into the newer cabins ,but we love it just the same.
Chris and his wife and staff went above and beyond to make our stay comfortable even replacing our cabin bbq on the same day it had just stopped working.
The staff took great care of my son who had a few falls.
Giving medical attention when needed.
Been to alot of Parks down the south , originally having parents who owned a van at Lake conjola in the 80s and 90s.
40yrs of being a south Coast holiday lover.
I can say hands down the best park and great activities supplied both in the park and out sourced.
Thanks very much to Chris and his wife and staff ,once again for an enjoyable and memorable holiday.
See you next Xmas and new years.
Just remember to all who stay and visit the park,
It's a really kid friendly park, and they are everywhere on bikes etc.
Do the 8km speed limit to keep us all safe and able to enjoy the holiday worry free.
Cheers.
Great check in, the customer service was awesome, superb facilities, perfect location and all at a decent price. What isn’t there to like about Holiday Heaven (and that’s not a typo! 😉)
Great atmosphere and within walking distance to town. Facilities clean and modern with very helpful hosts.

Great caravan park in a  great location. Close to everything. Ensuite sites for motor homes were a good size, the ensuite immaculately clean. The  units were comfortable and clean. Good park facilities and grounds

Stayed at an unpowered site for a few days and loved it here. Such an amazing site. Restrooms and showers were very clean and nicely kept up. A lovely kitchen/amenities area. Perfect location. What else could you ask for? Definitely worth visiting.

We had a powered site for our motor home at holiday haven Ulladulla to test it out ,awesome place top location walking distance to everything with awesome amenities so clean and very well run ,a great experience
Great location for access to town and boating. Clean amenities with friendly and helpful staff

Loved this spot honestly a wow !! Beautiful view , great for the kids , great spot to catch some fishing , would come back 😊,,would be great if there was a washing machine in the house though, despite the laundry outside the cabin , internet was down the next day not sure why ,but on the other hand great great spot for the kiddies,, great pool , jumping pillow and also the water park was really lovely,,loved how the showers near the pool had hot water for showering felt like an actual bathroom ,, showers in the cabin great also . Loved loved heaps !! 🙂🙂🙂🙂🙂🙂

I originally sent my feedback directly to the park but after no response I decided to do a review.|Booked in July for Sun 13 & Mon 14 August, and paid in full for 2 adults, as park requires payment 30 days prior to arrival, even in low season. Changed dates by phone  but wasn't told $20 extra until I received an email. No problem but I forgot to pay it and it was cancelled a few days later. Had to call again to sort it out, and was told it was the system that cancelled the booking. |Checked in on my own and the receptionist was lovely. My son arrived the following day, and was charged an extra $15, don't know why but we were running late for dinner so didn't worry about it. |The bed was very hard, needed a mattress topper. The shower head was very close to the wall and not adjustable, the water pressure was also a bit sad. We put up with a very odd smell in the cabin as we were out with family most of the time. We first thought it was gas leaking but it could have been a dead animal under the cabin.|It's a nice place to stay, the price was reasonable and the cabin was quite new and well appointed, it just wasn't that comfortable. |

A wonderful stop for a peaceful night’s rest. The park is very well run with good security, clean facilities and quiet evenings. The headland has impressive views through beautiful old gum trees, and there are excellent short walks to fossil rocks, a seawater swimming pool and nice shops nearby. Piper at reception was welcoming, friendly, kind and super efficient. I recommend this spot to everyone but it’s especially great value for those on a budget. Don’t forget to check out Mollymook beach and the lighthouse!

We have had an amazing holiday in Ulladulla thank you so much for your outstanding hospitality
The young girl,at the reception desk needs to be nominated for employee of the month for her wonderful work ethics and friendliness was out standing.
Lovely tranquil park with lots of shady trees. Clean amenities and friendly staff.
